title: Antarctica could warm 1.4 times faster than the rest of the Southern Hemisphere in the coming decades, study finds
summary: Antarctica could warm much faster than its surroundings over the next few decades due to a phenomenon known as polar amplification that is well established in the Arctic.

title: Watch sperm whale headbutt another for no apparent reason
summary: Researchers have captured extraordinary footage of sperm whales randomly headbutting each other, confirming anecdotal reports from mariners and whalers in the 18th and 19th centuries.

title: Urfa Man: An 11,500-year-old life-size statue of a man holding his penis
summary: Discovered in a city in southern Turkey, this life-size male statue may represent an important deceased ancestor.

title: A new twist on matter? Strange 'Half-Mӧbius' molecule has rare properties chemists have never seen before
summary: Chemists created a strange "half-Mӧbius" molecule, where electrons twist freely out of place to make a continuously looping surface.

title: Why do some people still believe that aliens shaped ancient civilizations?
summary: Two archaeologists explore the enduring myth that extraterrestrials contributed to the various ancient cultures around the world.

title: A secret weapon to fight carbon emissions was just discovered: Beavers
summary: A new study in Switzerland finds that beaver-built wetlands can trap and store large amounts of carbon, offering a low-cost boost for restoration and climate resilience.

title: Rare star spotted in its original galaxy could answer a key question about the ingredients of life: Space photo of the week
summary: The ancient and rare star PicII-503 is helping astronomers understand how carbon became so abundant in the universe.

title: Why do animals have different pupil shapes?
summary: Pupil shapes vary widely across the animal kingdom, from the W-shaped pupils of cuttlefish to the bar-like pupils of goats. But why are animal pupils shaped so differently?

title: Physicists created an electron 'catapult' that moves particles at 'extraordinary' speed
summary: Using a new method, physicists found a way to "catapult" electrons across solar materials in quadrillionths of a second.

title: Why are humans the only species with a chin?
summary: Potential explanations abound, yet recent research has shed new light on the question.

title: NASA's Artemis II rocket rolls to launch pad in final bid to meet April deadline
summary: A series of leaks means the coming launch window will be NASA's final attempt to meet its April mission deadline.

title: Human-driven climate change is slowing Earth's rotation at a rate not seen in 3.6 million years
summary: Today's sea level rise is significant enough to slow the rotation of the planet by just over a millisecond per century.

summary: Archaeologists have unearthed a Celtic cemetery in France that holds 18 unusual seated burials.

title: 'Dark oxygen' discovery on the seafloor is 'fundamentally at odds with thermodynamics' and should be retracted, experts say
summary: In a recent opinion article, marine scientists and electrochemists listed a number of reasons why it's unlikely that metallic nodules on the deep seafloor could produce oxygen in total darkness.

title: Best APS-C and Micro Four-Thirds cameras for astrophotography
summary: We've rounded up the best APS-C and Micro Four Thirds cameras for users who prefer a more compact and lightweight setup for astrophotography.

title: Divers find marble treasure from Athens' Acropolis in Lord Elgin's shipwrecked brig at the bottom of the Aegean Sea
summary: Divers investigating a shipwrecked brig once owned by Lord Elgin have discovered an overlooked piece of marble from the Acropolis in Athens.

title: All 5 'letters' of DNA found on an asteroid speeding through our solar system. What do they tell us about the origins of life?
summary: JAXA samples reveal that asteroid Ryugu has a complete set of nucleobases, the building blocks of DNA, suggesting these ingredients of life may be common in the solar system.

summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is sending humans back to the moon for the first time since the Apollo era. Here are the reasons why the U.S. wants to return.

title: Dark message warning enemy to 'learn your lesson' found inscribed on 2,000-year-old sling bullet from ancient Holy Land
summary: Archaeologists have discovered a 2,000-year-old lead bullet in the Holy Land with a darkly sarcastic inscription in Greek.

title: 'Parasites of human societies': How did we end up so close to cats?
summary: In the book "The Call of the Honeyguide," applied ecologist Rob Dunn explores mutually beneficial relationships between different species. But Dunn argues the relationship between humans and cats may force a rethink of what shape these mutualisms can take.

title: Rare 'daytime fireball' meteor creates powerful sonic boom as 7-ton space rock explodes above eastern US
summary: A fridge-size space rock spectacularly broke apart over Ohio at 40,000 mph, creating a loud boom and a "fireball" that shone in the bright blue daytime sky. The rare sight, which exploded with the equivalent force of 250 tons of TNT, was also seen from space.
